claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 20 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 20, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 20, which is 20 or over
circumstances: Child is 20, which is 20 or over
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit. To do this, I'll need to gather some information about your situation and any children you're claiming for.

Let me start with the first question:

**Do you have any children you'd like me to assess for Child Benefit?** If so, could you please tell me their names and ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have one child - Alex, who is 20 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I appreciate you providing Alex's details.

Before I can assess eligibility, I need to understand the rules better. Let me look up the specific criteria for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Based on the information I've reviewed, I can see that Child Benefit eligibility depends on several factors when a child is 16 or over.

Since Alex is 20 years old, I need to ask you some follow-up questions:

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training (such as university, college, or a recognised apprenticeship)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in any education or training at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. Based on the information I've gathered:

**Alex is 20 years old and is not in approved education or training.**

According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules I've reviewed, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is 20 years old and not in approved education or training, **Alex does not meet the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit**.

Let me now provide you with my formal outcome:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Outcome: Not eligible for Child Benefit**

Unfortunately, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex. The main reason is that Alex is 20 years old and is not currently in approved education or training. Child Benefit eligibility for children aged 16 and over requires them to be in approved education or training to qualify.

If Alex were to return to approved education or training in the future, you may be able to claim Child Benefit again. I'd recommend contacting the Child Benefit Office if your circumstances change or if you have any further questions.

reasonings: Alex is 20 years old. According to the eligibility rules, Child Benefit can normally only be claimed for a child under 20 if they stay in approved education or training. Alex is not currently in any approved education or training, so does not meet the eligibility criteria.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 20 years old and not in approved education or training. As Alex exceeds the age threshold and is not in approved education or training, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex."]

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 20 years old. According to the eligibility rules, Child Benefit can normally only be claimed for a child under 20 if they stay in approved education or training. Alex is not currently in any approved education or training, so does not meet the eligibility criteria.
